verdict_reasoning: SRC (proto-oncogene tyrosine kinase) is canonically an N-myristoylated, non-receptor kinase anchored exclusively to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via its SH4 domain lipid modification and polybasic cluster — no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no outer-leaflet lipidation. Under baseline conditions it is inaccessible from the extracellular face. However, a 2026 Science study (Zhou et al.) demonstrated that in cancer cells, Src undergoes topological inversion onto the outer/extracellular face of the plasma membrane via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE) — a cancer-state-specific, noncanonical secretory pathway. This outer-leaflet surface pool was detected both in vitro and in vivo, and was proposed as a therapeutic antibody target. This constitutes direct, gene-specific experimental evidence of cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation) outer-leaflet display, satisfying the contextual / cell_state_induced criterion. No evidence of germline-restricted display, lysosomal exocytosis under non-cancer conditions, or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering.
